featuretablefilter 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • featuretablefilter Tutorial: Complete Guide to Microbiome Feature Table Filtering

    Plot presence frequency analysis for features and samples

    plot_presence_analysis.Rd

    Creates histograms showing: (1) in how many samples each feature is present (feature prevalence), and (2) how many features are present in each sample (sample richness).

    When table_filtered is provided, generates side-by-side comparison plots with shared axes.

    plot_presence_analysis(table, threshold = 1,
                        save_dir = NULL, prefix = "presence_analysis",
                        main_main = "Presence Frequency Analysis",
                        width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300,
                        prev_bins = 20, rich_bins = 20,
                        table_filtered = NULL, prefix_filtered = "filtered")

    Arguments

    table

    A feature table (data.frame or matrix) with features as rows and samples as columns. First column should be feature IDs, remaining columns are sample counts.

    threshold

    Minimum abundance for a feature to be considered "present" in a sample. Default is 1 (any non-zero count).

    save_dir

    Optional directory to save plots. If NULL, plots are displayed only.

    prefix

    Prefix for saved plot filenames. Default is "presence_analysis".

    main_main

    Main title for the combined plot. Default is "Presence Frequency Analysis".

    width

    Plot width in inches for ggsave. Default 10.

    height

    Plot height in inches for ggsave. Default 6.

    dpi

    DPI for saved raster output. Default 300.

    prev_bins

    Number of bins for feature prevalence histogram. Default is 20.

    rich_bins

    Number of bins for sample richness histogram. Default is 20.

    table_filtered

    Optional filtered feature table for comparison. If provided, shows original vs filtered side by side with shared axes.

    prefix_filtered

    Prefix for filtered plot files. Default is "filtered".

    Value

    A list containing:

    feature_prevalence

    Named vector: number of samples each feature appears in (original)

    sample_richness

    Named vector: number of features in each sample (original)

    feature_prevalence_filtered

    Named vector: filtered feature prevalence (if table_filtered provided)

    sample_richness_filtered

    Named vector: filtered sample richness (if table_filtered provided)

    mean_feature_prevalence

    Mean number of samples per feature (original)

    mean_sample_richness

    Mean number of features per sample (original)

    mean_feature_prevalence_filtered

    Mean for filtered (if table_filtered provided)

    mean_sample_richness_filtered

    Mean for filtered (if table_filtered provided)

    min_feature_prevalence

    Minimum feature prevalence (original)

    max_feature_prevalence

    Maximum feature prevalence (original)

    min_sample_richness

    Minimum sample richness (original)

    max_sample_richness

    Maximum sample richness (original)

    min_feature_prevalence_filtered

    Minimum for filtered (if table_filtered provided)

    max_feature_prevalence_filtered

    Maximum for filtered (if table_filtered provided)

    min_sample_richness_filtered

    Minimum for filtered (if table_filtered provided)

    max_sample_richness_filtered

    Maximum for filtered (if table_filtered provided)

    n_features

    Total number of features (original)

    n_samples

    Total number of samples (original)

    n_features_filtered

    Total for filtered (if table_filtered provided)

    n_samples_filtered

    Total for filtered (if table_filtered provided)

    plots

    List containing the ggplot objects

    Examples

    # Display plots for original table only
# plot_presence_analysis(table)

# Compare original vs filtered (side-by-side with shared axes)
# plot_presence_analysis(table, table_filtered = filtered_table)

# Save plots
# plot_presence_analysis(table, save_dir = "plots", prefix = "my_analysis")

# Get statistics
# stats <- plot_presence_analysis(table)
# print(stats$mean_sample_richness)

    Contents
